# Questions in Livestream 245

Live Stream #245: Friday The 23rd

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=680" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:20</a> | Does overnight shipping exist in Canada? | Yes, he says Amazon offers overnight shipping in Canada, but it is usually expensive; free shipping tends to take several days. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=888" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:48</a> | If Ramesses later called for kidnapped Nubian labor, was that because the Hebrews had already left? | He says corvee labor from Nubia and Canaan was a normal New Kingdom practice and not necessarily a response to replacing Hebrews. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=1072" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:52</a> | How should one answer claims that Lorenzo Nigro has not shown the Jericho outer mudbrick wall is Late Bronze rather than Middle Bronze III? | He dismisses that pushback strongly and says the style of massive mudbrick fortifications fits Middle Bronze conventions, not the proposed revision. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=1241" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:41</a> | What does he think of Richard Steiner's recent Merneptah-related exodus proposal? | He rejects it outright as chronologically and textually untenable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=1537" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:37</a> | Is Christ's resurrected body physically somewhere in heaven, or in some unseen dimension? | He says heaven appears able to accommodate embodied humans, pointing to Enoch and Elijah as examples of bodily presence there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=1839" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:39</a> | Are donations to the channel tax deductible, and is PayPal better than Super Chat? | He says PayPal is better for the channel because it loses less to fees and is treated as a gift, while the donations are generally not tax deductible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=2082" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:42</a> | Should Christians claim tithes on their taxes when the law allows it? | Yes; he says believers should pay what they owe without cheating, but they should not overpay by refusing lawful deductions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=2260" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:40</a> | What is the context of Leiden 348, and why is it not talked about more? | He says the text in question is not the sensational "Habiru built Ramesses" proof some claim, and he starts by identifying it more carefully before drawing conclusions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=2892" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:12</a> | Is there legitimacy to the dual-causality principle in the Old Testament and ancient Near Eastern literature? | He says he is not really qualified to answer the philosophical side and treats his response as limited rather than definitive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=3396" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:36</a> | Will God publicly reveal people's earthly deeds like a movie after death? | Not like a movie, in his view; he says there will be an accounting that shows God's judgments are just and that rewards and punishments differ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=3799" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:19</a> | What does it mean to "work out your salvation" in Philippians 2:12-13 if believers are already saved? | He says the passage is about living out salvation's implications with proper nuance, not denying that believers already belong to Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=4427" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:47</a> | Are the achievements of Cain's line in Genesis 4:17-24 evil? | No; he says the achievements themselves are not evil, though some are retrojections and Cain's line is contrasted with Seth's line. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=4859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:59</a> | How accurate are the reconstructed rules of Senet? | He says they are not accurate at all because nobody actually knows how Senet was played. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=4911" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:51</a> | Do modern nations have angelic princes like Daniel 10:13 describes? | Yes; he identifies them with spiritual powers that influence nations and governments rather than directly possessing rulers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=5020" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:40</a> | If all power and authority have been given to Christ, why are these powers still in charge? | He says God permits evil for now, comparing it to the wheat and tares, and that Christ defeats these powers gradually. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=5174" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:14</a> | Do these spiritual beings have names and individual wills over the nations they influence? | To a greater or lesser extent, yes; he says spirit beings have names and wills, though they control countries only partially. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=5364" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:24</a> | How does one defeat or conquer such spiritual powers? | He says people cannot directly defeat them; they may destroy movements or nations they influence, but the powers move on. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=5621" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:41</a> | Does God speak to people today like the prophets of old, or is that only a figure of speech? | He says God can speak today, but modern claims should be treated carefully and do not carry the same weight as biblical prophecy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=5881" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:01</a> | How can someone legitimately confirm whether a person is a prophet today? | Compare the claim to Scripture; he says God's word does not contradict itself, so claimed revelation must be judged against prior revelation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=6105" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:45</a> | How did today's mainline Protestants get hijacked? | He says theological and cultural factors contributed, especially approaches that let other authorities override Scripture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=6251" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:44:11</a> | Is Ezekiel seeing the literal form of the cherubim? | No; he says Ezekiel is perceiving something real, but his mind and cultural framework supply imagery for spirit beings that are not literally bodies with wings and faces. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=6576" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:36</a> | Do ghosts tend to haunt places where witchcraft or occult practice has happened? | No; he says hauntings are not limited to occult sites and could reportedly occur in places with no such connection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=6641" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:41</a> | Can ghosts or spirits be caught on videotape or another recording device? | Generally not; he says ghosts do not interact with the electromagnetic spectrum in a way a camera or recorder could capture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=6827" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:47</a> | When were angels created? | He says the answer depends on whether "angel" means any good spirit or specifically a messenger, and that "when were spirit beings created?" is the clearer question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=7060" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:40</a> | Where can someone read more about primordial god deities? | He says no one has really written the needed monograph, and the only source he can point to at length is his own fiction treatment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=7214" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:14</a> | In Exodus 21:22-25, to whom is the death penalty relevant: the mother, the child, or both? | He reads the initial case as miscarriage without further injury, which brings a fine; if the woman is also injured or killed, lex talionis applies, including life for life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=7567" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:07</a> | Is Exodus 21:22 about miscarriage or premature birth? | He says it is miscarriage, though the miscarried child could be alive or dead in the scenario. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=7802" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:02</a> | Why did the risen Christ not appear to enemies such as Pilate, since that might have helped Christianity grow? | He says appearing to enemies would not guarantee belief; people can reject even strong evidence, so Christ appeared first to the faithful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=8021" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:41</a> | Is Arioch of Ellasar in Genesis 14 Rim-Sin of Larsa? | Probably not; he says Rim-Sin's dates do not fit the likely chronology, though another local king could still be possible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=8347" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:07</a> | How should one respond to someone who says he hears God and does not need or trust the Bible? | He calls that a formula for error and heresy, saying any claimed spiritual revelation must be checked against Scripture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=8616" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:36</a> | Would he consider doing reaction videos to real paranormal-story podcast channels if sponsored? | Yes, if properly sponsored; he says such projects would need to be arranged through Discord and meet his consulting-rate threshold. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=8930" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:50</a> | What kinds of sins do people commit unknowingly or without realizing they are sins? | He says it depends on upbringing, but gives examples such as sexual sin, "white lies," gossip, slander, and other normalized behavior. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=9235" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:55</a> | What are his thoughts on Winger's Bethel revelations? | He sides with Winger's criticism and describes Bethel-related practices as false prophecy, bad translation work, and marketing-like spirituality. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=9720" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:00</a> | Do Job's complaints count as prayer since he is basically talking to God? | Mostly no; he treats many of them as poetic laments, though Job's direct speech to God near the end could count as prayer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=9822" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:42</a> | What would count as a prayer? | Talking to God and making appeals to God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=9847" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:07</a> | Did the Egyptians record the seven-year famine from the Bible? | No; he says famines were common enough in Egypt that they were not consistently recorded, making identification difficult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=9951" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:45:51</a> | Did Job really happen, or is it just a story? | He says it is probably both: a real Job and historical core presented through mytho-history and poetic dramatization. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=10123" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:43</a> | What were the reasons behind Akhenaten's monotheism, if he was a monotheist? | He says Akhenaten was not a monotheist but a pantheistic monolatrist, driven partly by hostility toward Egypt's priesthoods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=10634" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:14</a> | Did God answer Cain about whether he was his brother's keeper? | No; he says God exposes Cain's misdirection instead, because Cain is not merely Abel's keeper but Abel's killer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=10785" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:45</a> | Did ancient Israelites view Satan the same way later Christianity did? | No; he says the Old Testament often uses "satan" as a job title for an adversary, while later Christianity treats Satan more as a proper name. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=11010" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:30</a> | How can a man control inappropriate dreams or night emissions while sleeping in order to be right with God? | He says they cannot be controlled, are part of normal male biology, and carry no moral guilt because there is no premeditation or intent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=11415" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:15</a> | What does he think about John Walton's view that the Mosaic law is wisdom literature? | He calls it a gross oversimplification and says he has reservations about Walton's later work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=11655" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:15</a> | Could older scholars drift in their views because they lose inhibitions or feel pressure to produce novelty? | He says people often become more of what they already were, and that prior compromise can become greater compromise with age. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Qs-d4ght8aU?t=11936" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:56</a> | What ethnicity or country would Jesus have considered himself? | He says Jesus would have considered himself Judean, a Jew from the tribe of Judah and line of David. |
